columns.description: Designated police boundary where offense(s) occurred. There are three police boundaries: reporting area, district and precinct. District is the middle size. A district can represent one contract city or unincorporated area, or multiple districts together can make up one contract city. There is a corresponding district shape file that can be used to associate an offense to a district.

description: The King County Sheriff’s Office (KCSO) is providing offense report data captured in it's Records Management System (RMS) from 2020 to present. KCSO replaced its RMS in late 2018 and at the same time transitioned to the FBI’s National Incident-Based Reporting System (NIBRS). The NIBRS standardization of crime classifications allows for comparison over time and between agencies. For official KCSO NIBRS reporting, please visit the WASPC Crime in Washington Report: https://www.waspc.org/cjis-statis

columns.name: Depth (m)

columns.name: RDL
columns.description: Reporting detection limit (see DataReadMeFile_WQ for more details)

columns.name: Sample Info
columns.description: Text entered by laboratory analysts, specific to the sample

columns.name: Steward Note
columns.description: Text entered by secondary data reviewers

columns.name: Replicates
columns.description: Indicates if there are replicate samples

columns.name: Data Source
columns.description: Who the data were analyzed by

columns.name: Sample ID
columns.description: Unique identifier to link samples collected from the same bottle

columns.name: Value
columns.description: Result

columns.name: Lab Qualifier
columns.description: Qualifier applied by laboratory analysts (see DataReadMeFile_WQ for definitions)

columns.name: QUALIFIER
columns.description: Laboratory qualifiers for King County Environmental Lab data. Qualifiers in this dataset include “H”, “SH”, “R”, “<MDL”, “<RDL”, ”RDL”, and “TA”.  General: "H" indicates that a holding time criterion (preservation, filtration, preparation, or analytical) was not met prior to completion of analysis; "SH" indicates that a sample handling criterion was not met in some manner prior to analysis. The sample may have been compromised during the sampling procedure or may not comply with storage conditions or preservation requirements; “R” indicates that the numerical result for the parameter, based on the professional judgment of the laboratory, is not scientifically defensible nor representative of the true value for a sample. Indefensible and non-representative data occur whenever sample collection, sample handling, critical analysis conditions or other system failures occur that would prevent the use of the result for any purpose other than an approximate minimum or maximum value; “<MDL” is applied when a target analyte is not detected or detected at a concentration less than the associated method detection limit (MDL). MDL is defined as the lowest concentration at which an analyte can be detected. The MDL is the lowest concentration at which a sample result will be reported; “<RDL” is applied when a target analyte is detected at a concentration greater than or equal to the associated MDL but less than the associated reporting detection limit (RDL). RDL is defined as the lowest concentration at which an analyte can reliably be quantified. The RDL represents the minimum concentration at which method performance becomes quantitative and is not subject to the degree of variation observed at concentrations between the MDL and RDL. “RDL”  is applied when a target analyte is detected at a concentration that, in the raw data is equal to the RDL; “TA” is applied to a sample result when additional narrative information is available in the text field. The additional information may help to qualify the sample result but is not necessarily covered by any of the standard qualifiers.

columns.name: RDL
columns.description: The RDL represents the  minimum concentration at which method performance becomes quantitatively accurate to the expected  capability of the method and is not subject to the higher degree of variation observed at concentrations between the MDL and RDL.

columns.description: Client locators are unique sample identifiers containing information about the sample’s storm event, collection timepoint, type, experimental group, column replicate, and process point. Client locators are written using the following sequence of identifying parameters: Storm_timepoint_type_experimentalGroup_rep_processpoint. Definitions of identifying parameters - Storm: Name of storm event (S1, S2, S3). Timepoint: Experimental collection timepoint (T0, T1A, T1B, T1C, T2, T3, T4). Type: T=treated by biofiltration, U=untreated.  Experimental group: I5 = stormwater taken directly from I5 TAPE site; INF = stormwater influent once composited into one tank; 60:40 = effluent from 60:40 BSM; HP1 = effluent from HPBSM type 1; HP2 = effluent from HPBSM type 2; HP3 = effluent from HPBSM type 3.  Rep: replicate identifier for bioretention columns (A, B, or C).  Process point: G=grab sample; E=effluent, EC=composite of effluent samples from replicate columns (A, B, and C) of same treatment.

columns.description: Three letter codes representing the fishes caught at each transect. A blank cell indicates that no fish were caught in that transect. Species codes: BBH = brown bullhead catfish (Ameiurus nebulosus); BCP = black crappie (Pomoxis nigromaculatus); BGS = bluegill sunfish (Lepomis macrochirus); CHK = chinook salmon (Oncorhynchus tshawytscha); CHM = chum salmon (Oncorhynchus keta); COH = coho salmon (Oncorhynchus kisutch); COT = cottids (Cottus sp); CUT = coastal cutthroat trout (Oncorhynchus clarkii clarkii); DAC = unknown dace (Rhinichthys sp); GSF = green sunfish (Lepomis cyanellus); LMB = largemouth bass (Micropterus salmoides); LMP = unknown lamprey; LND = long nose dace (Rhinichthys cataractae); LSS = large scale sucker (Catostomus macrocheilus); MIN = unknown minnow (Cyprinid); MWF = mountain whitefish (Prosopium williamsoni); NPM = northern pike minnow (Ptychocheilus oregonensis); PIN = pink salmon (Oncorhynchus gorbuscha); PMM = peamouth minnow (Mylocheilus caurinus); PSS = pumpkinseed sunfish (Lepomis gibbosus); RBT = rainbow trout (Oncorhynchus mykiss); RKB = rock bass (Ambloplites rupestris); RSS = redside shiner (Richardsonius balteatus); SPD = speckled dace (Rhinichthys osculus); STH = steelhead (Oncorhynchus mykiss); SUN = unknown sunfish (Lepomis sp); TRT = unknown trout; TSB = three-spine stickleback (Gasterosteus aculeatus); YLP = yellow perch (Perca flavescens).
